jokes.text: Hey, you mind keepin' it down?
jokes.setup: Someone complaining about noise to the main characters
jokes.punchline: The complaint itself, suggesting the characters are being disruptively loud
jokes.explanation: Simple setup joke that establishes character behavior through neighbor complaint

jokes.text: What? Now he's got a wacky neighbor?
jokes.setup: Character commenting on the neighbor situation
jokes.punchline: Self-aware joke about sitcom tropes - the 'wacky neighbor' character
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor acknowledging sitcom conventions within the show itself

jokes.text: No, maybe you should get out more. / Hmm, maybe we should.
jokes.setup: Defensive comeback suggesting someone else needs to get out
jokes.punchline: Immediate agreement that they both should get out more
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion where the comeback accidentally leads to agreement

jokes.text: It's supposed to be very expensive. / Mm, I don't know. It sounds expensive.
jokes.setup: Someone noting a restaurant is supposed to be very expensive
jokes.punchline: Second person expressing concern that it sounds expensive, redundantly
jokes.explanation: Simple absurdist humor through redundant observation about price

jokes.text: ♪ Just go already! ♪
jokes.setup: Extended discussion about going out
jokes.punchline: Impatient musical outburst telling them to just go
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with musical format adding to the exasperation

jokes.text: Baby, I'm the entire party.
jokes.setup: Confusion about 'rest of party' at restaurant
jokes.punchline: Bender's egotistical declaration that he is the whole party
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender line - combines wordplay with his narcissistic character

jokes.text: The Queen of Yonkers... ...Billionairebot, And Daytime Emmy nominee Calculon!
jokes.setup: Listing celebrities at the restaurant
jokes.punchline: Mix of absurd titles including 'Queen of Yonkers' and 'Daytime Emmy nominee'
jokes.explanation: Good world-building humor with absurd celebrity titles

jokes.text: I am a demanding actor, so I must order off the menu.
jokes.setup: Calculon introducing himself as demanding
jokes.punchline: His demand is to order 'off the menu' (meaning off-menu items)
jokes.explanation: Classic Calculon pompousness applied to restaurant ordering

jokes.text: Hmm, I don't see anything inconveniencing enough.
jokes.setup: Calculon looking at the off-menu menu
jokes.punchline: He wants something that inconveniences the restaurant staff
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Calculon's self-centered demanding nature

jokes.text: Yo, we're tryin' not to be noticed right here. / How come you didn't notice us?
jokes.setup: Criminals saying they're trying not to be noticed
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking why they weren't noticed, contradicting their goal
jokes.explanation: Great contradiction showing the mafia's illogical thinking

jokes.text: Not so loud. We're criminals!
jokes.setup: Characters being loud about not being noticed
jokes.punchline: Loudly declaring they're criminals while asking for quiet
jokes.explanation: Good irony in loudly declaring criminal status while wanting to be quiet

jokes.text: Market price. / Well, that doesn't sound so bad. / That's why we say it that way.
jokes.setup: Customer asking about truffle price, waiter saying 'market price'
jokes.punchline: Waiter admitting they phrase it deceptively to hide the high cost
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about restaurant pricing tactics with honest admission

jokes.text: Bam, bam, bam, bam, bam, bam, bam, bam, bam. / You missed a spot. / Bam?
jokes.setup: Elzar enthusiastically shaving truffles with his catchphrase
jokes.punchline: Customer pointing out he missed a spot, Elzar's confused single 'Bam?'
jokes.explanation: Good use of Elzar's signature move being interrupted by pedantic correction

jokes.text: I don't even have a sense of taste and these are incredible!
jokes.setup: Bender praising food
jokes.punchline: He admits he can't taste anything while calling food incredible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - enthusiastic about food he literally cannot taste

jokes.text: So your total is $83 for the food... That seems reasonable. ...plus $5,000.10 for the truffles, which brings us to a grand total of somethin' like $30,000.
jokes.setup: Normal food bill that seems reasonable
jokes.punchline: Massive truffle upcharge that somehow becomes $30,000 total
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing restaurant markup absurdity, though math is deliberately wrong

jokes.text: But-- But-- But I can't afford that! / No? Well then you're gonna have to wash dishes for a month. / Whoa! Whoa, whoa, whoa. No one wants to see that.
jokes.setup: Standard 'wash dishes to pay bill' trope
jokes.punchline: Bender's disgusted reaction implies Fry would be terrible/gross at dishwashing
jokes.explanation: Subverts dishwashing trope by focusing on how unpleasant Fry would be to watch

jokes.text: Well, deal! I mean, many years from now? I might be dead before then. / Yes. Or right around then.
jokes.setup: Fry optimistically thinking he might die of natural causes before favor is called in
jokes.punchline: Mafia ominously suggesting they might kill him around that time
jokes.explanation: Dark humor twist on Fry's naive optimism about avoiding consequences

jokes.text: They were indescribable. / That's exactly how I would describe 'em!
jokes.setup: Fry saying truffles were indescribable
jokes.punchline: Bender saying that's exactly how he'd describe them, missing the paradox
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay joke about the logical impossibility of describing something as indescribable

jokes.text: But of all the things I've shoved in my mouth without knowing what they were, they were definitely the best.
jokes.setup: Fry praising the truffles
jokes.punchline: Casual admission he regularly eats unknown substances
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry character moment - disgusting personal habit revealed casually

jokes.text: They're a fungus, you boobs! / You're a fungus!
jokes.setup: Professor calling them boobs and explaining truffles are fungus
jokes.punchline: Bender's childish 'you're a fungus' comeback
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective childish comeback that fits Bender's immature side

jokes.text: I'm so bored, I'm in pain!
jokes.setup: Professor about to give scientific explanation
jokes.punchline: Bender's extreme reaction to anticipated boredom
jokes.explanation: Great economy of language expressing Bender's dramatic intolerance for education

jokes.text: Yum! What is a truffle, anyway? / For God's sake, this is your ninth movie and you never know a damn thing. How did you get this job? / 'Cause you're my father.
jokes.setup: Educational film character asking basic question after many movies
jokes.punchline: Reveals he only has the job due to nepotism
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about incompetent actors in educational films and nepotism

jokes.text: Look, Papa, it's a foul swine! / And his trusty pig!
jokes.setup: Neville pointing out what appears to be a person
jokes.punchline: Father clarifying it's actually a person AND a pig, subverting expectation
jokes.explanation: Nice misdirection where the insult is actually about a person, not the pig

jokes.text: Just don't move into our neighborhood. / Seriously.
jokes.setup: Encouraging people to get rich from truffles
jokes.punchline: Immediately adding classist caveat about not wanting them as neighbors
jokes.explanation: Sharp social commentary on wealthy hypocrisy about social mobility

jokes.text: I heard Neville died from eating Pop Rocks and Coke.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation transition
jokes.punchline: References the urban legend about Pop Rocks and Coke being fatal
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference callback to childhood urban legend, somewhat cheap

jokes.text: I am off to ze Truffle Belt to seek my-- how you say-- fortunini? / That's not how you say it. / That's how I say it!
jokes.setup: Bender attempting fake French accent and making up word
jokes.punchline: Defending his made-up word when corrected
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bender's stubborn refusal to admit mistakes

jokes.text: And I thought he was rude before he was French.
jokes.setup: Bender adopting French persona
jokes.punchline: Comment about French people being characteristically rude
jokes.explanation: Relies on national stereotype for humor, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: Where ze 'ell are you supposed to be from? / Uh, France? / Never 'eard of eet. / Uh, 'Frahnce'? / Ah, oui, Frahnce! Love that place.
jokes.setup: Bender's bad French accent being questioned
jokes.punchline: Pronunciation correction makes all the difference to acceptance
jokes.explanation: Accent humor with absurd premise that slight pronunciation change matters

jokes.text: You get own pig! Oink, oink, oink. / Wow, can I have your talking pig? / All ze pigs can talk here!
jokes.setup: Bender wanting the pig
jokes.punchline: Revelation that ALL pigs can talk, making request less special
jokes.explanation: Good world-building absurdity that undercuts Bender's amazement

jokes.text: I don't understand. Frenchmen are usually so welcoming.
jokes.setup: French farmer being rude and unwelcoming
jokes.punchline: Bender's sarcastic comment about French hospitality stereotypes
jokes.explanation: Sarcastic take on French stereotypes, somewhat predictable

jokes.text: I'll start the bidding at negative $1. Negative $1. Will you take negative one? Negative one? No, I'll go down to two. Two, negative two dollars? Do I hear negative two? No. How 'bout negative five?
jokes.setup: Auction for terrible pig
jokes.punchline: Auctioneer bidding negative amounts and going lower
jokes.explanation: Creative subversion of auction format with negative bidding escalation

jokes.text: What is that, a shaved gerbil?
jokes.setup: Looking at tiny pig
jokes.punchline: Confused for completely different small animal
jokes.explanation: Simple size humor, visual gag setup

jokes.text: Alright! I'm gonna call you Jambone. / Wow! A name. Like in the movies.
jokes.setup: Bender giving the pig a name
jokes.punchline: Pig's innocent amazement at having a name like movie characters
jokes.explanation: Sweet character moment establishing Jambone's innocent personality

jokes.text: You smell any money? / You mean truffles? / Oh, right, I forgot there was a middle step.
jokes.setup: Bender asking pig to smell money
jokes.punchline: Bender forgetting truffles are the intermediate step to money
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender logic - wanting to skip to the end result without process

jokes.text: Oh, I really, really want to eat a truffle, but I'm not allowed to eat a truffle, so I can't eat a truffle.
jokes.setup: Jambone explaining why he needs the nose guard
jokes.punchline: Repetitive explanation showing his simple but earnest nature
jokes.explanation: Establishes Jambone's struggle with temptation through repetitive speech pattern

jokes.text: Zut alors!
jokes.setup: Bender and Jambone presumably doing something destructive
jokes.punchline: French exclamation suggests they've caused damage to competitor
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag reaction, relies on French exclamation for humor

jokes.text: I would hardly call this rustic.
jokes.setup: Earlier promised rustic scenery
jokes.punchline: Scene is actually industrial/destructive
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between expectation and reality

jokes.text: That's it. No one spits on my equipment! They may have a big fancy truffle truck, but I've got... Uh... Ah crap, I don't have anything!
jokes.setup: Bender's angry declaration about having something to compete with
jokes.punchline: Immediate realization he has nothing to compete with
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of heroic moment showing Bender's actual helplessness

jokes.text: At the current exchange rate? Uh, 3,000.
jokes.setup: Bender asking how much smackaroonies are in dollars
jokes.punchline: They're exactly the same amount, making the currency name pointless
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist humor about meaningless currency names

jokes.text: Ten lousy smackaroonies?
jokes.setup: Bender expecting to be rich from $3000
jokes.punchline: Cartel takes massive cut leaving him with almost nothing
jokes.explanation: Good deflation showing how cartels exploit workers

jokes.text: What will they shave on their entrées? / Let them shave cake.
jokes.setup: Jambone asking about rich people's truffle needs
jokes.punchline: Bender's twist on Marie Antoinette's 'let them eat cake'
jokes.explanation: Excellent historical reference adaptation that fits perfectly with truffle context

jokes.text: That's the most remote asteroid in the whole belt. But ain't no one what went never returned. / I'd like to think it's 'cause they died in agony. / Why do you like to think that? / 'Cause I'm psychotic!
jokes.setup: Ominous warning about dangerous asteroid
jokes.punchline: Cheerful admission of being psychotic for enjoying others' suffering
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor with honest character admission about sadistic tendencies

jokes.text: Why, that one there's more different from all the rest put together. / Which one? / This one. / That one? / That one!
jokes.setup: Vague description of special asteroid
jokes.punchline: Confusing pointing exchange
jokes.explanation: Standard pointing confusion comedy, mildly effective

jokes.text: Hey, what are you doing? Frolickin'?! You cut that out!
jokes.setup: Jambone playing in the soft environment
jokes.punchline: Bender's outrage at innocent frolicking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender character moment - angry at innocent joy and fun

jokes.text: No trees?! But you promised me riches beyond belief! I'm leavin'.
jokes.setup: Discovering there are no visible trees for truffles
jokes.punchline: Bender's immediate tantrum and abandonment of quest
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender impatience and blame-shifting when things get difficult

jokes.text: Gravity's kinda low. We can start yelling again when we get closer to the ground.
jokes.setup: Falling through low gravity
jokes.punchline: Practical consideration about when screaming becomes appropriate
jokes.explanation: Good practical absurdity about appropriate timing for panic

jokes.text: Perfect. You start lookin' for truffles while I complain about how long it's taking you.
jokes.setup: Arriving at truffle hunting ground
jokes.punchline: Bender openly admitting his plan to be unhelpful and complaining
jokes.explanation: Excellent Bender character moment - honest about being lazy and critical

jokes.text: Found one. / Already? Well, then I'll complain about how small it is. / Whoa, this is so big, it's gonna be hard to complain about, but I'll try.
jokes.setup: Bender planning to complain about truffle size
jokes.punchline: Truffle is huge but Bender commits to complaining anyway
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation showing Bender's commitment to negativity regardless of reality

jokes.text: Whoa, this is so big, it's gonna be hard to complain about, but I'll try.
jokes.setup: Large truffle found
jokes.punchline: Bender committed to complaining regardless
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character consistency, Bender will find something to complain about

jokes.text: I love you, Jambone! / I love you too, Mr. Bender. / Shut up, piggy. I know it.
jokes.setup: Emotional moment of mutual affection
jokes.punchline: Bender immediately shutting down the sentiment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender emotional defense mechanism - expressing love then rejecting reciprocation

jokes.text: If you're here to see the chair, it'll be 10 bucks. / We're not here to see the chair. / Hold on, boss. It's a pretty nice chair.
jokes.setup: Fry trying to monetize Bender's chair as memorial
jokes.punchline: Mafia member genuinely interested despite being there on crime business
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Clamps getting distracted by furniture

jokes.text: But you said that wouldn't happen for many years. / I find people worry less when I say it that way. Who needs the stress? It's a silent killer!
jokes.setup: Fry pointing out the contradiction in timing
jokes.punchline: Mafia boss admitting to lying to reduce anxiety while mentioning stress kills
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback with ironic concern for victim's stress levels from criminal

jokes.text: It doesn't have a bone. It's boneless. / Well, worn to a stump then.
jokes.setup: Bender threatening to work Jambone's snout to the bone
jokes.punchline: Jambone's literal correction and Bender's practical adjustment
jokes.explanation: Simple literalism joke with practical threat adjustment

jokes.text: Mr. Bender? Remember not to fall on a thorn.
jokes.setup: Earlier warning about thorns
jokes.punchline: Jambone helpfully reminding Bender as he's presumably falling
jokes.explanation: Good timing on callback with Jambone's earnest helpfulness

jokes.text: Maybe to hide from a worser monster?
jokes.setup: Bender asking why monsters look like other things
jokes.punchline: Jambone's innocent theory with incorrect grammar
jokes.explanation: Character moment showing Jambone's simple speech patterns and innocent nature

jokes.text: Maybe it's a nice monster.
jokes.setup: Scary monster appears
jokes.punchline: Jambone's hopeful optimism about dangerous situation
jokes.explanation: Classic innocent character hoping for best in obviously bad situation

jokes.text: Even if it contains no truffles, it's a nice bag. I'll use my clamps.
jokes.setup: Clamps ordered to confiscate bag
jokes.punchline: He's more interested in the bag itself and mentions his signature clamps
jokes.explanation: Good Clamps character moment - always finding excuse to mention his clamps

jokes.text: It was me! I told 'em. / Now can I get my clothes back?
jokes.setup: Mystery of how mafia found them
jokes.punchline: Fry betrayed them and is apparently naked
jokes.explanation: Good revelation with implied visual gag about Fry's nakedness

jokes.text: We take turns. It's common courtesy!
jokes.setup: Discussion of mafia favors
jokes.punchline: Clamps applying politeness rules to organized crime
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of criminal behavior with social etiquette

jokes.text: Really? That's horrible.
jokes.setup: Jambone saying Bender is the kindest person he's met
jokes.punchline: Bender's deadpan realization this reflects poorly on Jambone's life
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor - Bender recognizing the sad implications of being the 'kindest'

jokes.text: Three whole weeks!
jokes.setup: Jambone saying he spent his whole life chasing truffles
jokes.punchline: Bender's correction that it was only three weeks
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of dramatic statement with practical reality check

jokes.text: I will never, ever respect you if you don't snarf that fungus.
jokes.setup: Jambone refusing to eat the truffle
jokes.punchline: Bender's ultimatum about respect based on food consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender priorities - respect entirely based on following his advice

jokes.text: After that first taste, I never had a chance, genetically engineered as I was to crave them.
jokes.setup: Jambone explaining his transformation
jokes.punchline: Reveals his addiction was genetically programmed, making it inevitable
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark twist on addiction narrative - it was literally engineered into him

jokes.text: After that first taste, I never had a chance, genetically engineered as I was to crave them.
jokes.setup: Jambone explaining his addiction
jokes.punchline: Reveals the cruel irony of his genetic programming
jokes.explanation: Dark commentary on exploitation, genetic programming irony

jokes.text: You disgust me, sir, which is difficult to do. Bravo!
jokes.setup: Hedonismbot reacting to Jambone's gluttony
jokes.punchline: Praising Jambone for achieving something impressive (disgusting Hedonismbot)
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hedonismbot moment - praising someone for out-disgusting him

jokes.text: Might I suggest some juicy truffle-fed pork?
jokes.setup: Guests asking what to eat now
jokes.punchline: Suggestion to eat Jambone himself
jokes.explanation: Dark humor turning the character into the meal solution

jokes.text: And if we eat one of our own, why then, we are no better than skinny people.
jokes.setup: Hedonismbot defending Jambone from being eaten
jokes.punchline: His moral reasoning is based on not being like skinny people
jokes.explanation: Brilliant twisted moral logic - cannibalism is wrong because it's what thin people would do

jokes.text: Thank you. I'm deeply honored and ashamed.
jokes.setup: Jambone receiving the Glutton Award
jokes.punchline: Simultaneous honor and shame about the achievement
jokes.explanation: Perfect emotional complexity for character who achieved what he never wanted
